title: Inconsistent packet_hash prefix between Python sim and Rust hetero_node
description: Python uses hashlib.sha256(payload).hexdigest()[:16] (first 8 bytes as hex str). Rust uses first 16 bytes of binary Sha256 digest for packet_hashes_sent/received sets. This breaks cross-implementation interoperability verification via packet hashes in hetero fleet tests and metrics. Violates test integrity (inconsistent oracles across impls) and correctness for sim metrics. See simulation.py:606, hetero_node.rs:145,176. P1 bug.
close_reason: Fixed packet_hash to consistently use 16-char hex prefix from sha256 hexdigest()[:16] across Python and Rust (local closure in hetero_node.rs, record methods with cap, full hash lists in METRICS JSON). Performed 3 codereview passes by filing/fixing project-LICHEN-6o8m, project-LICHEN-v536, project-LICHEN-5do8. Cargo check/clippy clean. Interop/metrics now consistent.
